auth.usage_instructions: Use `grammarly auth` for manage grammarly api authentication. Available subcommands here are `login`, `logout`, `status`, `test`, `profiles`.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly auth login` to configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent. Common options: `--profile/-p`, `--force/-F`, `--credential-type/-c`.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly auth logout` to clear stored credentials. Common options: `--profile/-p`.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `grammarly auth profiles` for manage authentication profiles. Available subcommands here are `list`, `get`, `create`, `set-default`, `delete`.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly auth profiles create` to create a new profile from .env.example template. Required arguments: `NAME`.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly auth profiles delete` to delete a profile and its data. Required arguments: `NAME`. Common options: `--force/-F`.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly auth profiles get` to get details for a specific profile. Required arguments: `NAME`. Common options: `--table/-t`.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly auth profiles list` to list all profiles and show which is the default. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.
status.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly auth status` to check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--profile/-p`.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly auth test` to test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--profile/-p`.

cache.usage_instructions: Use `grammarly cache` for manage response cache. Available subcommands here are `clear`.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly cache clear` to remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.

docs.usage_instructions: Use `grammarly docs` for manage grammarly documents. Available subcommands here are `list`, `get`, `read`, `new`.

get.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly docs get` to get a document by ID. Required arguments: `DOCUMENT_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`.

list.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly docs list` to list Grammarly documents. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

new.help: Create a new document. Opens a URL to create a new Grammarly document. Note: Grammarly creates documents client-side via URL navigation. This command outputs the URL to open.
new.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly docs new` to create a new document. Opens a URL to create a new Grammarly document. Note: Grammarly creates documents client-side via URL navigation. This command outputs the URL to open. Options: `--title/-t`.

read.help: Read document content (text only). Returns the document with its full text content.
read.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly docs read` to read document content (text only). Returns the document with its full text content. Required arguments: `DOCUMENT_ID`.

plagiarism.usage_instructions: Use `grammarly plagiarism` for plagiarism detection commands. Available subcommands here are `check`, `status`.

check.help: Check a document or text for plagiarism. Submit a file or text for plagiarism detection. By default, waits for the result (polls until COMPLETED or FAILED).
check.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly plagiarism check` to check a document or text for plagiarism. Submit a file or text for plagiarism detection. By default, waits for the result (polls until COMPLETED or FAILED). Required arguments: `FILE_PATH`. Common options: `--table/-t`.

options.name: --max-wait
options.help: Maximum seconds to wait for result

status.help: Get the status of a plagiarism check. Check the status and result of a previously submitted plagiarism detection request.
status.usage_instructions: Run `grammarly plagiarism status` to get the status of a plagiarism check. Check the status and result of a previously submitted plagiarism detection request. Required arguments: `SCORE_REQUEST_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`.

arguments.name: score_request_id
arguments.help: Score request ID from a previous check

usage_instructions: Use for cli interface for grammarly api. Commands follow `grammarly <group> <action>`. Primary groups here are `grammarly auth`, `grammarly plagiarism`, `grammarly docs`, `grammarly cache`.
